Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WARNER BROS. DISTRIBUTING INC.'s Motion To File Cross-Complaint.

Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To File Cross-Complaint is GRANTED.

Leave to file a cross-complaint "may be granted in the interest of justice at any time during the course of the action." (Code of Civil Procedure section 428.50(c).) "A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith." (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99.)

Good cause appearing, the court grants Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc. leave to file the proposed cross-complaint. Defendant Warner Bros. Distributing, Inc. must file its cross-complaint, substantially identical to Exhibit A to the Khoder Declaration, within seven calendar days of entry of this order.

Moving Party must prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the time set for hearing.

A proposed order is submitted for the court's review and signature.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-27-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT 15/40 PRODUCTIONS LTD. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment

Appearances (by Zoom):
Joshua White, Esq. 415-988-9828 Altair Law for Plaintiff. (In-person)
Stephan Choo, Esq. 415-972-6303 Ropers Majeski for Defendant 15/40 PRODUCTIONS LTD.
Court reporter: Rosa Samples, CSR #12383, 909-262-7351.

ARGUED. Mr. Choo sent a timely notice to contest: "Defendant met its burden of demonstarting the nonexistence of any triable material facts as to the existence of open and obvious condition and that it was not foreseeable for Plaintiff to encounter the condition." Mr. Choo presents arguments. Court and Counsel look at photos of fork/forklift truck.

Mr. White responds to "open and obvious hazard" - photographs, Defendant did not shift burden, camouflaged tripping hazard, expert's testimony, "foreseeability". Mr. Choo responds.

After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 15/40 PRODUCTIONS LTD.'s Motion For Summary Judgment.

Defendant's motion for summary judgment is taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Rosa Samples, CSR #12383, 909-262-7351. Reported. (302/JMQ)

***After the hearing, the court signs an Order Denying Defendant's motion for summary judgment is signed. (see scanned order for details)***
